 {"id":"franken_networkx-k88","title":"Add is_chordal, chromatic_number approx, and additional parity functions","description":"Add is_chordal (via perfect elimination ordering), approximate chromatic_number (via greedy coloring upper bound), and graph_number_of_cliques for quick parity improvements.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-21T08:21:13.700499226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:29:32.012915753Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:29:32.012208133Z","close_reason":"Added is_chordal (MCS-based PEO detection) in Rust + Python. Matches NetworkX on all tested graphs (complete, cycle, path, star, wheel). Also added greedy_color strategy parameter.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-kdpx","title":"NX-REPLACE: Graph generators delegating to NX — random_labeled_tree, triad_graph, named generators","description":"## Functions (5) — graph generators delegating to NX
1. random_labeled_tree (__init__.py:3130) — we now have Rust from_prufer_sequence!
2. triad_graph (__init__.py:6446) — named 3-node triad subgraph
3. random_powerlaw_tree_sequence (__init__.py:8299) — power-law degree sequence
4. random_unlabeled_rooted_forest (__init__.py:6911) — random forest generation
5. barabasi_albert_graph with initial_graph param (__init__.py:2486) — BA model with custom initial

## Implementation plan
- random_labeled_tree: generate random Prüfer sequence of length n-2, call our Rust from_prufer_sequence. Simple!
- triad_graph: hardcoded 16 triad types, each is a 3-node directed graph. Simple lookup table.
- random_powerlaw_tree_sequence: generate sequence using power-law distribution, verify graphical.
- random_unlabeled_rooted_forest: complex random generation — may keep NX.
- BA with initial_graph: need to convert initial_graph to Rust format or handle in Python.

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_generator_delegations_parity.py:
- test_random_labeled_tree_is_tree: verify is_tree(random_labeled_tree(n))
- test_random_labeled_tree_seeded: verify deterministic with seed
- test_random_labeled_tree_matches_nx_structure: same n, both produce valid trees
- test_triad_graph_all_types: verify all 16 triad types have correct edge sets
- test_triad_graph_matches_nx: compare each type against NX
- test_powerlaw_tree_sequence_is_graphical: verify is_valid_degree_sequence
- test_ba_with_initial_graph: verify initial subgraph preserved

Run: pytest tests/python/test_generator_delegations_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewrite 3-4 functions
- tests/python/test_generator_delegations_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:39.952431758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:30:37.919458477Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:30:37.919195099Z","close_reason":"Completed native/parity generator tranche; split exact native random_unlabeled_rooted_forest to follow-up bead","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-kdpx","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-okpa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:21.121029139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-kf07","title":"Readwrite: reject empty attr keys in edgelist","description":"Treat empty attr keys as malformed in read_edgelist; strict fails closed, hardened warns and drops attr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:10:10.005842266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:11:12.835414600Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:11:12.835087704Z","close_reason":"Completed: empty attr key now fail-closed in strict, warn in hardened","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-kg11","title":"PARAM: Implement 'default' param for view .get() and 'keys' for matching functions","description":"## Problem: 2 minor parameter gaps
1. View .get() methods (views.rs:117,233; digraph.rs:2737,2839) ignore default — always return None
2. min_edge_cover/min_weight_matching (algorithms.rs:3217,3274) ignore keys param for MultiGraphs

## Fix plan
1. Views: in .get() impl, check if key exists; if not return default instead of None
2. Matching: when keys=True, include edge key in returned tuples for MultiGraph

## Testing requirements
Add to tests/python/test_view_params_parity.py:
- test_node_view_get_default: G.nodes.get(missing, 'fallback') == 'fallback'
- test_node_view_get_existing: G.nodes.get(existing) returns data dict
- test_edge_view_get_default: similar for edges
- test_min_edge_cover_keys_true: on MultiGraph, returns 3-tuples
- test_min_weight_matching_keys_true: on MultiGraph, returns 3-tuples

Run: pytest tests/python/test_view_params_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-python/src/views.rs: implement default param
- crates/fnx-python/src/digraph.rs: implement default param
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: implement keys param
- tests/python/test_view_params_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:52.867414847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:41:45.324704312Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:41:45.324426485Z","close_reason":"Implemented default parameter for nodes(data='attr', default=val) and edges(data='attr', default=val) on Graph, DiGraph, and their views. Added AttrWithDefault variant to NodeViewData and ViewData enums. Works for both node and edge views, with and without nbunch filtering. 7 parity tests added in test_view_default_parity.py. Note: 'keys' param for matching functions deferred (MultiGraph edge keys are a separate concern).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
